ref: Then he said to the servants, “It is time for the wedding banquet, and the invited guests don't deserve to come.
text: Kɛkɛ nɛ e de e tsɔli ɔmɛ ke: ‘A to yo kpeemi ɔ he ni gbe nyɛ mohu, se nihi nɛ a kpee ɔ, a sɛ́.’
translated: The old man said to him: ‘If you want to marry my daughter, you must first bring me a human head, and then I will give her to you.’

ref: Jesus used stories when he spoke to the people. In fact, he did not tell them anything without using stories.
text: Abɛhi a mi Yesu gu kɛ tu munyu nɛ ɔmɛ tsuo kɛ tsɔɔ asafo ɔmɛ, nɛ abɛhi a mi sɔuu kɛkɛ e kɛ mɛ tuɔ munyu ngɛ;
translated: When Jesus saw the crowd that was following him, and when he saw that they were hungry, he had compassion on them;

ref: After Jesus left the crowd and went inside, his disciples came to him and said, “Explain to us the story about the weeds in the wheat field.”
text: Kɛkɛ nɛ Yesu je asafo ɔmɛ blɔ nɛ e sɛ we ɔ mi. Nɛ e kaseli ɔmɛ ba e ngɔ ba de lɛ ke: “Tsɔɔ wɔ ngmɔ ɔ mi hu ɔ he abɛ ɔ sisi.”
translated: Then Jesus went to the synagogue and taught. And they brought to him a man who had a withered hand, and they asked him: “Is it lawful to heal on the Sabbath?”

ref: The high priest then tore his robe and said, “This man claims to be God! We don't need any more witnesses! You have heard what he said.
text: Kɛkɛ nɛ osɔfo nɔkɔtɔma a gba e tadehi nɛ e ke: “E bɔ musu! Mɛni wa pa ngɛ odase yeli hlae ya pee ekohu oʼɛ, jeha nɛ ɔ nyɛ nu e musu bɔmi ɔ!
translated: The pastor who was standing there said to him: “Be quiet! What do you have to do with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are—the Holy One of God!”

ref: Respect your father and mother. And love others as much as you love yourself.”
text: buu o tsɛ kɛ o nyɛ; kɛ suɔ o nyɛmi nɔmlɔ kaa bɔ nɛ o suɔ mo nitse o he.”
translated: The person who is coming is not the one who is; when the sun sets, the one who is will come and meet the person who is coming.
